name: JetBrains Air

runner.notes: Install the desktop preview, prepare a benchmark run, open only runs/<run-id>/worktree as a Local Workspace, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification. Record the selected guest agent, model, effort, authentication path, and permission mode separately from Air.

inclusion: Direct Class A ADE competitor: a standalone JetBrains environment for running heterogeneous coding agents concurrently, isolating their work, and reviewing changes with code intelligence.
pricing_model: Preview desktop product. JetBrains AI Pro or Ultimate can provide supported agents; users can also bring provider API keys or supported Codex/Gemini subscriptions. JetBrains AI Free and Enterprise are not supported by the current Air preview.
public_stack_signals: Standalone proprietary desktop application for macOS, Windows, and Linux; Linux distribution uses JetBrains Toolbox.
Built-in support for Codex, Claude Agent, Gemini CLI, and Junie, plus configurable ACP-compatible agents and local-model harnesses.
Tasks can run in the local workspace or isolated Git worktrees; Docker task execution is documented for macOS and Windows but not Linux.
Official workflow covers plan authoring, concurrent agent tasks, code-aware context, diff review, inline comments, and cross-agent review.
moat_hypotheses: agent-agnostic control plane with built-in and ACP agents
integrated worktree and Docker execution choices
setup_notes: Linux installation currently goes through JetBrains Toolbox.
Use Local Workspace for the first v0.1 benchmark so all edits remain inside the harness-owned worktree.
Do not count announced cloud execution, automation, or Air Team capabilities as current benchmark features.
Guest-agent quality can dominate single-task correctness; keep Air, agent, model, and subscription metadata separate.

sources.notes: Current positioning, built-in agents, concurrent task loops, workspaces, isolation choices, review workflow, subscription/BYOK options, and Linux distribution link.
Current preview status and downloads for macOS, Windows, and Linux via JetBrains Toolbox.

Release history for Linux/Windows support, ACP agents, local models, worktrees, Docker host support, planning, permissions, comments, agent review, and configuration files. Reviewed at version 262.132.21 dated 2026-07-15.
JetBrains launch article covering the ADE category, concurrent agents, code-aware context, local/worktree/Docker execution, review workflow, subscriptions, BYOK, and future-only cloud/team direction.
Official workflows for plan files, local or worktree tasks, parallel worktrees, diff review, and one-agent-reviews-another behavior.
Official ACP expansion, local-model support, Java/Kotlin code intelligence, proposed-change review, and Docker availability on Windows and macOS.

research.positioning: Standalone JetBrains agentic development environment for defining coding tasks with code-aware context, running heterogeneous agents concurrently in isolated workspaces, and reviewing their changes before they reach the main working copy.

frontend: code navigation and diff review surface
Java/Kotlin intelligence powered by the IntelliJ IDEA code engine in beta
packaging: Windows x64 and ARM64 desktop downloads
data: project-scoped workspaces with agent sessions and Git state
unknowns: The proprietary implementation languages, desktop framework, persistence format, and telemetry details are not established by the official sources reviewed.
Cloud execution and Air Team are announced future surfaces and are not counted as current capabilities.
Exact support depth varies by agent harness and must be recorded in hands-on runs.

infrastructure: Runs coding agents locally by default.
Can isolate concurrent tasks in separate Git worktrees.
Can execute Docker tasks on macOS and Windows when Docker Desktop is available; Docker tasks are unavailable on Linux in the reviewed release.
Supports ACP-compatible agents, including harnesses connected to local models through Ollama or similar runners.
Workspaces scope sessions, Git state, navigation, and tools to a project.

agent_support: OpenCode, Pi, Cline, and other configurable ACP agents
local models through an ACP-compatible harness
orchestration: Plan mode with an editable Markdown execution plan
one agent can review another agent's changes
workspace_isolation: Docker tasks on macOS and Windows
review_shipping: proposed-change inspection before file permission approval
Git client and generated commit-message assistance
apply reviewed worktree or container changes to the main copy
context_memory: task context from files, lines, symbols, classes, methods, commits, and images
integrations: provider API keys and supported personal subscriptions

install_surface: JetBrains Toolbox plus Air preview on Linux
JetBrains Account or supported provider authentication
prepared harness worktree opened as a Local Workspace
parity_risks: Guest-agent and model quality can dominate single-task correctness; record both separately from Air.
The Linux host cannot exercise Air's Docker task path in the reviewed release.
Air-created worktrees may live outside the harness-owned run directory; use Local Workspace for v0.1 scored tasks unless artifact capture is extended.
Current tasks mostly measure coding outcomes, not Air's parallel delegation and cross-agent review differentiators.
The product is moving quickly in public preview; record the exact Air version for every run.

hypotheses: JetBrains distribution and code-intelligence experience can reduce friction in task definition and review.
Built-in plus ACP agent breadth can make Air a neutral control plane rather than a single-provider environment.
Integrated local, worktree, and Docker choices can make concurrent task isolation accessible to desktop users.
Cross-agent review can turn heterogeneous agent choice into a quality-control workflow.
evidence: The official site and launch article position Air as a standalone ADE centered on multiple concurrent coding agents.
Official release notes document worktrees, Docker tasks, ACP agents, local models, plan files, permissions, diff comments, and agent review.
Official Windows workflow documentation shows plan-to-worktree-to-review and cross-agent review flows.
unknowns: Hands-on evidence is still required for install friction, worktree behavior, review ergonomics, and verifier-compatible delivery.
No public source repository establishes internal architecture or allows source-level security review.
The value of code intelligence outside Java/Kotlin needs hands-on verification rather than inference.
